The post CIVICUS External Learning and Community Design Consultancy 2026: Apply for a Global Consultancy to Build the WeRise Global Community appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
CIVICUS Global Alliance is inviting experienced consultants and consulting teams to submit Expressions of Interest (EOIs) for the External Learning and Community Design Consultancy 2026. This three-month consultancy presents an exciting opportunity for professionals specializing in community building, participatory design, organizational learning, and movement strengthening to help shape the future of the WeRise Global FoPA Solidarity Infrastructure.

The consultancy aims to transform an existing network of grassroots organizations into a sustainable, movement-owned global community that strengthens collaboration, learning, solidarity, and collective action beyond traditional funding relationships.

Application Deadline: 5 August 2026

About CIVICUS

CIVICUS is a global alliance dedicated to defending people power and strengthening civil society worldwide. With more than 17,000 members across over 180 countries, the organization works to:

  • Protect civic freedoms.
  • Strengthen civil society organizations.
  • Promote accountability.
  • Support grassroots movements.
  • Amplify voices from the Global South.
  • Build stronger global solidarity among civil society actors.

Through research, advocacy, partnerships, and capacity-building, CIVICUS works to ensure that citizens can organize freely and participate meaningfully in shaping their societies.

About the WeRise Initiative

Grassroots organizations worldwide continue to defend the right to peaceful assembly despite:

  • Shrinking civic spaces.
  • Increasing political repression.
  • Complex operating environments.
  • Funding limitations.
  • Fragmented support systems.

Through two successful WeRise cohorts, CIVICUS has already supported:

  • 32 grassroots organizations
  • Movement collectives across multiple regions
  • Learning exchanges
  • Innovation spaces
  • Grant support
  • Solidarity initiatives

As the current funding cycle concludes, CIVICUS aims to preserve the relationships, trust, and knowledge developed through the programme by creating the WeRise Global FoPA Solidarity Infrastructure.

Rather than remaining a donor-supported network, the vision is to establish a sustainable, movement-led community that promotes:

  • Cross-border collaboration
  • Shared learning
  • Mutual support
  • Collective advocacy
  • Long-term solidarity

Purpose of the Consultancy

The selected consultant will lead a participatory process that enables members of the WeRise network to co-create the future structure of the community.

The consultancy seeks to ensure that the future community is:

  • Member-owned
  • Sustainable
  • Inclusive
  • Collaborative
  • Built around the priorities of grassroots movements

Consultancy Objectives

The consultant will work closely with CIVICUS and community members to:

  1. Assess existing relationships, strengths, learning needs, and expectations across the WeRise network.
  2. Co-design the future vision and identity of the community.
  3. Develop governance structures and engagement models.
  4. Recommend sustainable collaboration mechanisms.
  5. Design a long-term solidarity infrastructure.
  6. Produce a practical roadmap for launching the WeRise Global Community.

Scope of Work

Successful consultants will undertake several key activities.

1. Community Discovery and Ecosystem Assessment

The consultant will:

  • Conduct participatory assessments.
  • Identify community strengths.
  • Map relationships.
  • Explore collaboration opportunities.
  • Assess members’ aspirations and priorities.

2. Community Listening Process

The consultant will design and facilitate an inclusive consultation process that:

  • Encourages broad participation.
  • Builds trust.
  • Ensures equitable representation.
  • Captures diverse perspectives.
  • Identifies priorities for the future community.

3. Co-create the Community Model

Working collaboratively with members, the consultant will help define:

  • Community purpose
  • Vision
  • Identity
  • Guiding principles
  • Governance model
  • Ways of working
  • Member ownership mechanisms

4. Develop the Community Value Proposition

The consultancy will identify the services and opportunities the community should provide, including:

  • Peer learning
  • Collaboration opportunities
  • Knowledge exchange
  • Solidarity initiatives
  • Capacity strengthening
  • Collective advocacy

5. Community Engagement Framework

The consultant will recommend systems for sustaining active member participation through:

  • Leadership development
  • Community governance
  • Learning opportunities
  • Collaboration models
  • Shared stewardship

6. Implementation Roadmap

A strategic roadmap will outline:

  • Launch phases
  • Governance recommendations
  • Priority actions
  • Community development milestones
  • Long-term sustainability strategies

Expected Deliverables

The selected consultant will produce:

  • Inception Report
  • Community Assessment Report
  • WeRise Community Design Framework
  • Community Implementation Roadmap
  • Final Consultancy Report

Required Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate experience in:

  • Movement building
  • Community organizing
  • Network facilitation
  • Participatory learning
  • Organizational learning
  • Communities of Practice
  • Social movements
  • Civic space
  • Human rights
  • Human-centered design
  • Systems thinking
  • Participatory design methodologies
  • Qualitative research
  • Report writing
  • Multi-country stakeholder engagement

Consultancy Duration

The consultancy will run for three months, from:

  • September 2026
  • October 2026
  • November 2026

Application Requirements

Interested consultants should submit:

  • A full Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  • A maximum two-page cover letter describing:
    • Relevant experience.
    • Technical expertise.
    • Proposed methodology.
    • Approach to delivering the consultancy.
  • A proposed consultancy budget.

Applications should be sent via email to FoPA@civicus.org  with the subject line:

EOI – External Learning and Community Design Consultancy

Why You Should Apply

This consultancy offers professionals an opportunity to:

  • Influence a global movement-building initiative.
  • Work alongside leading civil society organizations.
  • Design innovative community governance models.
  • Support grassroots movements worldwide.
  • Strengthen global civic participation.
  • Promote sustainable collaboration beyond donor funding.
  • Contribute to long-term systems change within international civil society.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 5 August 2026

Interested consultants are encouraged to submit complete applications before the deadline, as late submissions may not be considered.

Final Thoughts

The CIVICUS External Learning and Community Design Consultancy provides a unique opportunity for experienced facilitators, movement builders, and organizational learning experts to help shape a global community committed to defending civic freedoms and strengthening grassroots collaboration.

If you have experience in participatory design, community building, and international civil society, this consultancy allows you to contribute to a transformative initiative that will continue supporting movements long after traditional grant funding has ended.

For more information on this opportunity, visit here

Discover more global consultancy opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post CIVICUS External Learning and Community Design Consultancy 2026: Apply for a Global Consultancy to Build the WeRise Global Community appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Scholarship 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Research Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The University of Melbourne is inviting applications for an exciting Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) Research Degree Opportunity focused on Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities. Hosted by the School of Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences within the Faculty of Science, this interdisciplinary project offers aspiring researchers the opportunity to investigate how […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarship 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Research Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Melbourne is inviting applications for an exciting Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) Research Degree Opportunity focused on Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities. Hosted by the School of Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences within the Faculty of Science, this interdisciplinary project offers aspiring researchers the opportunity to investigate how biodiversity access affects the wellbeing of disadvantaged urban communities.

Students admitted to the project will automatically be considered for a range of University of Melbourne scholarships, making this an excellent opportunity for candidates seeking funded doctoral studies in Australia.

The project is expected to commence on 1 February 2027 and will be based at the University’s Burnley Campus in Melbourne, Australia.

About the PhD Project

Cities around the world continue to expand rapidly through urbanization. While urban development brings economic opportunities, it also increases the separation between people and the natural environment. As cities grow, access to biodiversity—including parks, native vegetation, wildlife habitats, and green spaces—becomes increasingly unequal.

This research project aims to understand how accessibility to biodiversity differs among underprivileged urban communities and how unequal access influences physical, mental, and social wellbeing.

Using an interdisciplinary approach that combines ecological science, geography, urban planning, spatial analysis, and social research, the project seeks to generate evidence that can support more equitable and sustainable city planning.

Research Focus

The PhD research will investigate several important questions, including:

  • How biodiversity is distributed across urban neighbourhoods.
  • Which communities have the greatest access to biodiversity.
  • How socioeconomic disadvantage affects biodiversity exposure.
  • The relationship between biodiversity access and community wellbeing.
  • Strategies that governments and planners can adopt to improve equitable access to urban biodiversity.

The research will specifically focus on Melbourne, Australia, serving as a case study with global relevance for sustainable cities.

Why Biodiversity Matters

Biodiversity offers numerous benefits that extend beyond environmental conservation.

Research has shown that access to biodiverse environments can contribute to:

  • Improved mental health
  • Reduced stress and anxiety
  • Better physical health
  • Lower disease risks
  • Enhanced environmental education
  • Stronger community wellbeing
  • Increased resilience to climate change
  • Better quality of urban life

Despite these benefits, biodiversity-rich environments are often concentrated in wealthier neighbourhoods, leaving disadvantaged communities with fewer opportunities to experience nature.

This project seeks to address these inequalities through evidence-based research.

Research Methodology

The successful PhD candidate will apply multiple research approaches, including:

Social Research

  • Community engagement
  • Surveys
  • Interviews
  • Socioeconomic analysis

Ecological Research

  • Biodiversity assessments
  • Species distribution analysis
  • Habitat evaluation

Spatial Analysis

  • Geographic Information Systems (GIS)
  • Urban mapping
  • Accessibility modelling
  • Spatial data analysis

This interdisciplinary methodology will generate practical recommendations for urban planners and policymakers.

Degree Awarded

Successful applicants will pursue a:

Doctor of Philosophy (PhD)

at the University of Melbourne.

Scholarships

Applicants are automatically considered for a range of University of Melbourne scholarships.

Some additional scholarships may require separate applications.

Scholarship opportunities may include support for:

  • Tuition fees
  • Living allowance
  • Research costs
  • Relocation assistance (where applicable)

Applicants are encouraged to review scholarship requirements on the University of Melbourne website.

Research Fields

The project aligns with several internationally recognized research disciplines, including:

  • Human Geography
  • Ecology
  • Urban and Regional Planning
  • Ecological Applications

Supervisory Team

The successful candidate will work under the supervision of leading researchers:

Principal Supervisor

  • Marie C. Dade
    • School of Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences

Internal Co-supervisor

  • Ilan Wiesel
    • School of Geography, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences

Host Institution

The research will be undertaken at:

University of Melbourne

Faculty of Science

School of Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences

Burnley Campus

Melbourne, Australia

Project Start Date

The anticipated commencement date is:

1 February 2027

Prospective students are encouraged to submit applications early to allow sufficient time for admission and scholarship processing.

Why You Should Apply

This PhD offers an outstanding opportunity to:

  • Conduct interdisciplinary research addressing global sustainability challenges.
  • Work alongside internationally recognized researchers.
  • Gain advanced expertise in ecology, urban planning, geography, and environmental science.
  • Contribute evidence that can improve biodiversity equity in cities.
  • Be automatically considered for University of Melbourne scholarships.
  • Build an academic and professional career in environmental sustainability and urban resilience.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their application through the University of Melbourne Research Degree Opportunities portal.

Applicants should review:

  • PhD admission requirements
  • Scholarship eligibility
  • Research project details
  • Required supporting documents

Applications are encouraged well before the project start date to maximize scholarship opportunities.

Apply Here 

For more information on this scholarship opportunity, visit here 

Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarship 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Research Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
